instruction:
For each statement, express the null hypothesis $H_0$ and alternative hypothesis $H_1$ in symbolic form. 1. $\ $ At least one-half of all Internet users make on-line purchases. [ANS] A. $H_0: p \ge 0.5,$ $H_1: p < 0.5$  B. $H_0: \mu \le 0.5,$ $H_1: \mu > 0.5$  C. $H_0: \mu \ge 0.5,$ $H_1: \mu < 0.5$  D. $H_0: p \le 0.5,$ $H_1: p > 0.5$
2. $\ $ IQ scores of statistics students have a standard deviation at most 15. [ANS] A. $H_0: \sigma \ge 15,$ $H_1: \sigma < 15$  B. $H_0: \mu \le 15,$ $H_1: \mu > 15$  C. $H_0: \mu < 15,$ $H_1: \mu \ge 15$  D. $H_0: \sigma \le 15,$ $H_1: \sigma > 15$
3. $\ $ The mean salary of statistics professors is less than 70,000 dollars. [ANS] A. $H_0: \mu \ge 70,000,$ $H_1: \mu < 70,000$  B. $H_0: \mu > 70,000,$ $H_1: \mu \le 70,000$  C. $H_0: \mu < 70,000,$ $H_1: \mu \ge 70,000$  D. $H_0: \mu \le 70,000,$ $H_1: \mu > 70,000$
